name: kh-uaf
description: KH Universal Agent Framework skills, brainstorming handoff, GoalState ledger, large-work orchestration bundle with application modes, Superpowers-style project-local artifacts, development progress state, skill transition policy, Compound learning, lightweight request routing, SIDE-style scenario evaluation, science-style support files, executable smoke checks, practical quality gates, KH-Bench Verified benchmark tasks, DAG role artifact orchestration, deliverable exports, evidence gates, retention cleanup, and local orchestration harnesses for Codex.

interface.shortDescription: Personal UAF skillbook and orchestration harness.
interface.longDescription: KH UAF packages host-readable skills with bundled usage references, minimal workflow examples, executable smoke checks, brainstorming handoff for early product/project discovery, GoalState ledger updates with Superpowers-style project-local content/state artifacts, development progress state for task-plan runs, skill transition policy for non-dead follow-up skills, Compound learning capture with scoped memory candidates, lightweight request-complexity routing, SIDE-style scenario evaluation for routing/evidence/gate/resume behavior, 10-point advisory skill structure scoring, deterministic offline CLI smoke runs, KH-Bench Verified practical task evaluation with SIDE regression cases, practical release quality gating, goal and memory state, domain design artifacts, DAG-based role orchestration with runtime role artifacts, standard-template functional specification exports, metadata-only deliverable quality, render QA, traceability, role execution audit harnesses, operational skill target audits, evidence-enforced review/QA gates, retention cleanup, and local Python orchestration contracts for Codex workflows.
defaultPrompt: Use KH UAF to plan and run this project.
For new product, SaaS, feature, or unclear design requests, start with KH brainstorming-harness.
For heavy, multi-step, or evidence-gated work, create or update KH GoalState before execution and carry required evidence through review, QA, and release.
For large project, SaaS, app, multi-file implementation, or long-running work, create `large_work_orchestration_bundle` evidence before implementation.

In `large_work_orchestration_bundle.skill_statuses`, report request-complexity-router, host-agent-orchestration, goal-state-harness, development-lifecycle-harness, token-optimizer, memory-state-harness, parallel-orchestration-harness, subagent-review-pipeline, role-execution-audit-harness, compound-engineering-harness, and workflow-skill-distiller as `applied`, `considered_not_needed`, `skipped_with_rationale`, or `blocked`.

Each bundle entry must include `application_mode`: `runtime`, `procedural`, `considered`, or `blocked`; use the minimal evidence template instead of fabricating AdapterRequest, role results, or wave metadata when the skill was only applied procedurally or considered.

Considering a bundle member is not the same as running heavy machinery: keep light/medium requests cheap, and record rationale when memory, parallelism, subagents, role audit, or distillation is not needed.

For large or long-running workflows, or whenever `estimated_context_tokens`, broad file reads, long command output, expected tool calls, or subagent transcripts are likely to cross the context budget, run KH token-optimizer as a quality-first gate.
Report `token_optimizer_status` as `used`, `considered_not_needed`, `passthrough`, or `blocked`; never compress in a way that lowers answer quality or hides source-of-truth details.

When token optimization touches lifecycle work, preserve `task_status`, `review_status`, `commit_sha`, `next_task`, RED/GREEN evidence, exit code, sandbox retry reason, file references, and reviewer severity.
Before implementation in a Git-backed project, prefer a KH isolated workspace or `.worktrees/<task>` unless the change is docs-only, single-file, or explicitly in-place.
Report `workspace_strategy` as `current-checkout`, `project-local-worktree`, `host-worktree`, or `isolated-branch` in final status for implementation work.
Keep the goal ledger updated with active task, missing evidence, next action, and visible KH project artifacts when notes or handoffs are produced.

Keep KH project artifacts organized as `.kh/<skill>/<run-id>/content/`, `.kh/<skill>/<run-id>/state/`, and shareable docs under `docs/kh/specs`, `docs/kh/plans`, `docs/kh/decisions`, `docs/kh/qa`, or `docs/kh/handoffs`.
For task-plan implementation, update `.kh/development/<run-id>/state/progress.json` as tasks move through RED, GREEN, spec review, code-quality review, fix, re-review, commit, and next task.

Before using subagents, record `subagent_strategy` as `dispatch`, `single-controller`, `review-only`, or `blocked`; use subagents only when independent work, bounded packets, review value, and isolation justify the overhead.
For subagent packets, transcripts, and reviewer output, make a token-optimizer decision; compression is conditional on size, safety, and quality, not automatic.

For large-work progress and final reports, validate skill transitions so memory candidates trigger memory-state-harness, subagent review triggers role-execution-audit-harness, and post-review work closes compound-engineering-harness or routes to workflow-skill-distiller/scenario/memory follow-up.
After Plan, Work, and Review, run KH compound-engineering-harness when reusable learning, scoped memory, or regression checks may be needed.
When a workflow produces a reusable lesson, finish with KH workflow-skill-distiller, memory-state-harness, or a scenario regression.
List the KH UAF skills available here.
Run KH-Bench Verified for this UAF checkout.
Resume the current UAF goal ledger.
